tluaMetatableShadowed.tlua(12,22): error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type 'table'.
tluaMetatableShadowed.tlua(13,25): error TLUA2339: Property 'n' does not exist on type 'table'.
tluaMetatableShadowed.tlua(20,26): error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type '{ n: number; }'.


==== tluaMetatableShadowed.tlua (3 errors) ====
    // Only the real globals carry the protocol. A shadowing local, or a call through an alias,
    // resolves to a different symbol and keeps whatever it declares -- the rule the type()
    // narrowing builtins already follow.
    
    local Helpers = { describe = function(self: table): string return "d"; end };
    local mt = { __index = Helpers };
    
    function shadowed(): void
      local setmetatable = function(value: table, metatable: table): table return value; end;
      local getmetatable = function(value: table): table return value; end;
      local obj = setmetatable({ n = 1 }, mt);
      local absent = obj.describe;
                         ~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type 'table'.
      local ownAbsent = obj.n;
                            ~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property 'n' does not exist on type 'table'.
      local back = getmetatable(obj);
    end
    
    // An alias is a different symbol too.
    local alias = setmetatable;
    local aliased = alias({ n = 1 }, mt);
    local aliasBad = aliased.describe;
                             ~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type '{ n: number; }'.
    local aliasOwn: number = aliased.n;
    
    // The globals themselves still work in the same file.
    local real = setmetatable({ n = 1 }, mt);
    local d: string = real.describe(real);
